criteria.criterion: A1: Depressed mood most of the day, nearly every day
criteria.evidence: low mood

criteria.criterion: A2: Markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all or almost all activities most of the day (anhedonia)
criteria.evidence: anhedonia

criteria.criterion: A3: Significant weight loss/gain or appetite change
criteria.evidence: low appetite

criteria.criterion: A4: Insomnia or hypersomnia
criteria.evidence: poor sleep

criteria.criterion: A8: Diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness
criteria.evidence: poor focus

criteria.criterion: A: ≥5 of 9 symptoms during the same 2-week period, with at least one being depressed mood or anhedonia
criteria.evidence: Patient reports 5 symptoms (low mood, anhedonia, low appetite, poor sleep, poor focus), but duration of 2 weeks not yet reported

criteria.criterion: B: Symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning
criteria.evidence: No report of functional impairment yet

criteria.criterion: C: Episode not attributable to physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition
criteria.evidence: Cannot be determined from phone intake; requires clinical judgment

criteria.criterion: D: Episode not better explained by schizoaffective disorder, schizophrenia, schizophreniform disorder, delusional disorder, or other specified/unspecified schizophrenia spectrum and psychotic disorders
criteria.evidence: Cannot be determined from phone intake; requires clinical judgment

criteria.criterion: E: There has never been a manic or hypomanic episode (unless substance-induced or attributable to a medical condition)
criteria.evidence: No history of mania/hypomania reported yet

criteria.criterion: A: Depressed mood for most of the day, for more days than not, for at least 2 years (1 year in children/adolescents)
criteria.evidence: Low mood reported, but duration of at least 2 years not yet reported

criteria.criterion: B: Presence, while depressed, of two or more of: poor appetite or overeating; insomnia or hypersomnia; low energy or fatigue; low self-esteem; poor concentration or difficulty making decisions; feelings of hopelessness
criteria.evidence: low appetite, poor sleep, poor focus (3 symptoms reported)

criteria.criterion: C: During the 2-year period, the individual has never been without the symptoms in Criteria A and B for more than 2 months at a time
criteria.evidence: Persistence over time not yet reported

criteria.criterion: D: Criteria for a major depressive episode may be continuously present for 2 years
criteria.evidence: Cannot determine yet

criteria.criterion: E: There has never been a manic episode and criteria have never been met for hypomanic episode or cyclothymic disorder
criteria.evidence: No history of mania/hypomania reported yet

criteria.criterion: F: The disturbance is not better explained by a persistent schizoaffective disorder, schizophrenia, schizophreniform disorder, delusional disorder, or other specified or unspecified schizophrenia spectrum and other psychotic disorder
criteria.evidence: Cannot be determined from phone intake; requires clinical judgment

criteria.criterion: G: The symptoms are not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition
criteria.evidence: Cannot be determined from phone intake; requires clinical judgment

criteria.criterion: H: The symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning
criteria.evidence: No report of functional impairment yet

criteria.criterion: A: Excessive anxiety and worry (apprehensive expectation), occurring more days than not, for at least 6 months, about a number of events or activities
criteria.evidence: No report of excessive anxiety/worry or 6-month duration yet

criteria.criterion: B: The individual finds it difficult to control the worry
criteria.evidence: No report of difficulty controlling worry yet

criteria.criterion: C1: Restlessness or feeling keyed up or on edge
criteria.evidence: Not reported

criteria.criterion: C2: Being easily fatigued
criteria.evidence: Not reported

criteria.criterion: C3: Difficulty concentrating or mind going blank
criteria.evidence: poor focus

criteria.criterion: C4: Irritability
criteria.evidence: Not reported

criteria.criterion: C5: Muscle tension
criteria.evidence: Not reported

criteria.criterion: C6: Sleep disturbance (difficulty falling/staying asleep, or restless, unsatisfying sleep)
criteria.evidence: poor sleep

criteria.criterion: C: ≥3 of 6 symptoms (at least some present more days than not for past 6 months)
criteria.evidence: Only 2 symptoms reported (poor focus, poor sleep); 6-month duration not reported

criteria.criterion: D: The anxiety, worry, or physical symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning
criteria.evidence: No report of functional impairment yet

criteria.criterion: E: Not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition
criteria.evidence: Cannot be determined from phone intake; requires clinical judgment

criteria.criterion: F: Not better explained by another mental disorder
criteria.evidence: Cannot be determined from phone intake; requires clinical judgment
